text: In Lesson 1, you learned how drug addiction affects the brain. Why might a person keep using drugs even when they know the risks? Use details from the lesson to support your answer.

In Lesson 2, you learned how depressants and club drugs affect the brain and mood. Why might these drugs seem appealing to some people, and how can that lead to repeated use? Use examples from the lesson to support your answer.
Lesson 3 explained how narcotics affect the body and brain. What can lead people to misuse narcotics, and why can it be hard for them to stop? Use information from the lesson to support your answer.

Lesson 4 discussed how stimulants affect the body and brain. What are some risks of misusing stimulants, and why might people still choose to use them? Use details from the lesson to support your answer.

Lesson 5 introduced hallucinogens and inhalants. What are these substances, how do they affect the mind and body, and why might some people use them even though they are risky? Use information from the lesson to support your answer.

Lesson 6 explained the effects of marijuana on the mind and body. What are the main effects of marijuana, and why might some people choose to use it even though it has risks? Use information from the lesson to support your answer.

Lesson 7 focused on performance-enhancing drugs. Why might athletes or other people choose to use these drugs even though they can cause health problems and are unfair in sports? Use information from the lesson to support your answer.
Based on the lesson on tobacco use, what are two reasons people start using tobacco, and what are two health risks of using it? Use information from the lesson to support your answer.
Based on the lesson on vaping, what are two reasons people choose to vape, and what are two health risks of vaping? Use information from the lesson to support your answer.

Based on the lesson on alcohol use, what are two reasons people choose to drink alcohol, and what are two health risks of excessive alcohol use? Use information from the lesson to support your answer.

Your body constantly receives stimuli from the environment. How does the nervous system detect, process, and respond to a stimulus, such as a loud noise or a hot stove, so the body can react quickly and stay safe? Use specific concepts from the lesson to support your answer.
Lesson 1 focused on mental and emotional health. How can low self-esteem negatively affect a person's well-being? Use information from the lesson to support your answer.

Lesson 2 explained the importance of understanding emotions. Describe one situation in which fear can be helpful and one situation in which fear can be unhealthy. Use information from the lesson to support your answer.
Lesson 3 focused on managing stress. How can stress affect each side of the health triangle: physical, mental/emotional, and social health? Use information from the lesson to support your answer.
What are mental health disorders, and how can they affect a person's daily life? Describe possible causes, their impact on well-being, and why it is important to seek support and treatment.
What are the main roles of vitamins and minerals in the body? Explain how they support health, give examples of food sources, and describe why it is important to get a balanced amount of each.

What is the role of carbohydrates in a balanced diet? Explain how carbohydrates help the body and what makes them helpful or harmful depending on the type and amount eaten. Use information from the lesson to support your answer.
How do fats help the body, and what makes fats either healthy or unhealthy in a person's diet? Use information from the lesson to support your answer.

What is the role of protein in a balanced diet? Explain how protein supports the body and what can happen when a person gets too little or too much. Use information from the lesson to support your answer.

What are fad diets, and how can they affect a person's health? Describe their common features, possible short-term results, and long-term risks. Use information from the lesson to support your answer.
What are eating disorders, and how can they affect physical and mental health? Describe possible causes and explain why getting help is important for recovery.

In this lesson, you learned how the immune system fights pathogens. Imagine that a virus enters the body. Describe how the immune system would respond, including the roles of T-cells and B-cells. Use details from the lesson to support your answer.
Bacteria, fungi, protozoa, and viruses can all cause disease. Choose two of these and explain how each one spreads and one way people can prevent infection from each.
Why might HIV infection rates be higher in some developing countries than in developed countries? Use examples such as access to healthcare, education, or treatment to support your explanation.

Cancer happens when cells grow out of control. Explain how one lifestyle choice, such as smoking, too much UV exposure, or poor diet, can increase the risk of cancer, and describe one way a person can reduce that risk.
Based on the lesson on heart disease prevention, what are two actions people can take in everyday life to lower their risk of heart disease?
How does the endocrine system help control growth, metabolism, and stress to keep the body in balance, and what can happen when this system does not work properly?
